skill.name: gws-gmail-watch
skill.description: Gmail: Watch for new emails and stream them as NDJSON.
skill.summary: Monitor Gmail for new messages and receive real-time NDJSON output via Google Workspace CLI.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 20 potential security issues, all of which are false positives after manual review. The external_commands detections are documentation code blocks (bash examples), filesystem detections are markdown hyperlink references (../ paths), and weak crypto detections are false positives on version numbers. This is documentation-only content with no executable code.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Blocks Trigger Static Scanner
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 15 external_commands patterns in SKILL.md lines 16, 22-39, 43. These are markdown code blocks containing bash command examples (gws gmail +watch), not executable code. No actual command execution occurs.

low_findings.title: Markdown Hyperlinks Trigger Path Traversal Scanner
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 3 filesystem patterns at SKILL.md lines 16, 58, 59. These are relative path references in markdown links (e.g., ../gws-shared/SKILL.md) for navigation, not actual filesystem operations.

content.value_statement: Stop missing important emails. This skill helps you monitor Gmail in real-time and stream new messages as structured data for automated processing.
actual_capabilities: Watch Gmail inbox for new messages in real-time
Stream incoming emails as NDJSON format for parsing
Filter by Gmail labels (INBOX, UNREAD, custom labels)
Configure Pub/Sub topics and subscriptions for push notifications
Control message batching with max-messages and poll-interval flags
Write individual email messages to separate JSON files
limitations: Requires the gws CLI tool to be installed separately
Requires Google Cloud Platform project for Pub/Sub resources
Gmail watch subscriptions expire after 7 days and must be renewed
Only works with Gmail accounts via Google Workspace API
Pub/Sub resources persist unless --cleanup flag is used
Cannot modify or delete emails through this skill

use_cases.title: Automated Email Processing Pipeline
use_cases.description: Set up real-time monitoring to catch important emails and trigger automated workflows like extracting data from receipts, invoices, or notifications.

use_cases.title: Customer Support Response Monitor
use_cases.description: Watch for high-priority support emails and alert the team instantly when critical messages arrive in the support inbox.

use_cases.title: Personal Inbox Watchdog
use_cases.description: Monitor personal Gmail for specific senders or labels and receive NDJSON output for logging or analysis in productivity tools.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Inbox Watch
prompt_templates.prompt: Watch my Gmail inbox for new emails and show them as they arrive. I want to see the output in the terminal.
prompt_templates.scenario: Get started with real-time email monitoring

prompt_templates.title: Filter by Label
prompt_templates.prompt: Watch only unread emails in my Gmail INBOX label. Pull messages every 10 seconds and stop after finding 5 messages.
prompt_templates.scenario: Monitor specific label with custom polling

prompt_templates.title: Save to Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Watch my Gmail for new emails and save each message to a separate JSON file in the ./emails directory. Clean up resources when done.

prompt_templates.title: Use Existing Pub/Sub Subscription
prompt_templates.prompt: Connect to my existing Pub/Sub subscription at projects/my-project/subscriptions/email-sub and pull messages once.
prompt_templates.scenario: Integrate with already configured GCP infrastructure

output_examples.input: Watch my Gmail inbox for new emails
output: Setting up Gmail watch for INBOX...
Watching for new emails (press Ctrl-C to stop)...

output_examples.input: Watch unread emails once and exit
output: Run again to renew watch (expires in 7 days)

best_practices: Use the --cleanup flag when testing to avoid leaving orphaned Pub/Sub resources in your GCP project
Renew your Gmail watch every 7 days to maintain uninterrupted monitoring
Start with --once flag to test your configuration before running the continuous watcher
Specify --label-ids to filter messages and reduce noise from irrelevant labels
anti_patterns: Do not run without --cleanup in production tests unless you want Pub/Sub resources to persist
Do not set --max-messages too high as it may cause memory issues with large email batches
Do not forget to set up proper GCP project permissions before running the watch command
Do not use --poll-interval below 1 second to avoid Gmail API rate limiting

faq.question: What does this skill actually do?
faq.answer: This skill monitors your Gmail inbox and streams new emails as NDJSON data. It uses Google Workspace CLI to set up push notifications via Pub/Sub and pulls messages as they arrive.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the gws CLI tool installed. This skill requires an external binary that is not included with Claude Code.

faq.question: How long does the watch last?
faq.answer: Gmail watch subscriptions expire after 7 days. You must re-run the command to renew monitoring and continue receiving new emails.

faq.question: What is NDJSON output format?
faq.answer: NDJSON means Newline-Delimited JSON. Each email appears as a separate JSON object on its own line, making it easy to parse with scripts or data tools.

faq.question: Can I filter specific email labels?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --label-ids flag with comma-separated label names like INBOX,UNREAD, or custom label IDs to filter which emails trigger notifications.

faq.question: What happens to Pub/Sub resources when I stop?
faq.answer: By default, Pub/Sub topics and subscriptions persist for reconnection. Use --cleanup to delete them automatically when you exit the watcher.
